parallel--2126b1d1-a7ab-4284-9173-2f4ef7.name: Analyze process creation events to detect anomalous parent-child process relationships involving service management tools.

description: This is an automatically generated playbook based on the question: A detections playbook for T1543 of MITRE ATT&CK Matrix for Enterprise framework is required.The target platform is Windows. You can find more information at https://attack.mitre.org/detectionstrategies/DET0571#AN1575 . The description of the approach follows: Detects command-line or API-based creation/modification of Windows Services via `sc.exe`, `powershell.exe`, `services.exe`, or `ChangeServiceConfig`. Looks for creation/modification of autostart services via registry changes, file drops to `System32\services`, and anomalous parent-child process trees.

external_references.name: MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1543: Create or Modify System Process

items.content: Collect and monitor command-line arguments for processes such as sc.exe, powershell.exe, and services.exe for service creation or modification commands.
Monitor Windows API calls, particularly ChangeServiceConfig, for unauthorized or anomalous service configuration changes.
Monitor the Windows Registry for modifications to autostart service entries, especially in HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services.
Monitor file system writes to the %SystemRoot%\System32 directory, specifically for the creation or modification of service-related executable files.
Identify processes spawning sc.exe, powershell.exe, or services.exe from non-standard or unexpected parent processes.
Correlate process trees with user context and execution time to identify suspicious activity.
Analyze process creation events to detect anomalous parent-child process relationships involving service management tools.
Correlate detected events with known legitimate administrative activity to reduce false positives.
Generate alerts for any detected suspicious service creation, modification, or associated behaviors that deviate from baseline norms.
